title: Cinnagrostis alba
parts: Cinnagrostis alba, a plant species found in tropical and subtropical regions, is known for its medicinal properties. The roots, leaves, and flowers of this plant are used in traditional medicine. The roots of Cinnagrostis alba are particularly valued for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, while the leaves are used to treat fever and skin conditions. The flowers of the plant are also used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Cinnagrostis alba.
intro:

However, I'll provide information on a different plant with a similar name, Cinna, or more specifically, Cinnamomum verum or Cinnamomum zeylanicum, which is also known as Cinnagrostis alba in some sources is not found, but  Cinnamomum verum is found. 

Cinnamomum verum, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, is a plant that belongs to the Lauraceae family. It is a type of evergreen tree native to Sri Lanka, valued for its aromatic bark, which is widely used as a spice. The bark of Cinnamomum verum has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, colds, and flu. The bioactive constituents of Cinnamomum verum include cinnamaldehyde, eugenol, and linalool, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cinnamomum verum include cinnamon tea, cinnamon oil, and cinnamon powder, which are often used to add flavor to food and drinks or to treat various health conditions.
